content: User prefers tech-lead reviews to avoid bookkeeping-only "close tracking" actions. Resolved items should be recorded in summary (e.g., resolved-by-decomposition) and excluded from Priority Actions/Persistent Issues.
User wants items they mention to be added one-by-one into knowledge as backlog entries from now on.
Add capability for users to create and upload agents similarly to skills.
Support using tool plugins directly in TypeScript form without build/package step; reference pi-mono approach for detailed design.
Introduce domain-dedicated tech-lead reviewers (core, runtime, web, tui) instead of only whole-repo review, and run them on GitHub Runner for automated checks.
On GitHub Runner workflows, replace Claude usage with the Diligent binary as the execution engine.
In TUI, make the plan UI float fixed at the bottom of the viewport.

In the web client, clicking + New conversation should enter a draft state instead of eagerly creating a thread: keep URL at '/', keep sidebar list unchanged, and create/hydrate the actual thread only when the first user message is sent.
User prefers framing implementation scope as 'focused on the current task' rather than 'minimal changes'/'최소 수정'.
User wants stale or no-longer-valid knowledge entries to be actively updated or deleted rather than left in place.

Do not store immediately planned implementation intent as knowledge. Anti-pattern: adding a knowledge entry like 'user wants to build some feature' and then immediately saying 'now I will start implementing it.' Knowledge should capture durable preferences, patterns, corrections, discoveries, or actual backlog items worth preserving across sessions, not transient intent for the current turn.

Update the update_knowledge tool description to state that knowledge must only capture durable cross-session value (preferences, patterns, discoveries, corrections, or real backlog items), and must not store transient current-turn intent or immediate implementation plans. Anti-pattern example: storing 'user wants to build X' right before implementing X in the same turn.
In TUI, when resuming a conversation, do not print the entire past message history; show a bounded/condensed resume view instead.
In the web client, after the user answers a question/request_user_input prompt, the resulting tool block should be rendered in the conversation UI.
